properties.description: The daily time range (in UTC) during which ElastiCache begins taking a daily snapshot of the node group. Minimum a 60 minute period. Format: hh24:mi-hh24:mi

properties.name: subnet_group_name
properties.description: The name of the cache subnet group to be used for the replication group.

properties.name: transit_encryption_enabled
properties.description: Specifies whether to enable encryption in transit.

properties.name: transit_encryption_mode
properties.description: Specifies whether to use encryption in transit for the replication group.

properties.name: service_updates_enabled
properties.description: Enable or disable automatic service updates for the ElastiCache cluster. Default: true

properties.name: service_updates_types
properties.description: A list of the service update types that will be applied automatically for the replication group. Default: ['security-update', 'engine-update']

properties.name: service_updates_severities
properties.description: A list of the service update severity levels that can be applied for the replication group. Default: ['critical', 'important']

properties.name: service_updates_cooldown_days
properties.description: The number of days that ElastiCache will wait before applying a service update to the replication group. Default: 14 days for production, 7 days for staging, and 5 days for others.

nestedProperties.name: master_user_secret
nestedProperties.description:
Secret containing the user/password authentication data. It must only
contain the "master_user_name" and "master_user_password" keys

nestedProperties.name: admin_user_arn
nestedProperties.description:
The ARN for the admin user.
This option is mutually exclusive with admin_user_credentials.

nestedProperties.name: admin_user_credentials
nestedProperties.description:
Secret containing the user/password authentication data. It must only
contain the "master_user_name" and "master_user_password" keys.
The admin user password must be at least 8 chars long, contain at least one
uppercase letter, one lowercase letter, one number, and one special character.
This option is mutually exclusive with admin_user_arn.

aws/group-1.yml.description:
Schema for defining AWS groups. Groups represent collections of users
or entities within an AWS account, along with associated policies
and metadata.

properties.name: account
properties.description:
Reference to the AWS account associated with the group. This links
the group to its parent account.

properties.name: name
properties.description:
The unique name of the group. This name is used to identify the group
within the AWS account.

properties.name: description
properties.description: A detailed description of the group, explaining its purpose and scope.

properties.name: policies
properties.description:
A list of policies associated with the group. Each policy defines
permissions and access controls for the group.

nestedProperties.name: s3_bucket
nestedProperties.description: S3 bucket name where the plugin archive is stored

nestedProperties.name: s3_key
nestedProperties.description: S3 object key of the plugin archive

nestedProperties.name: s3_object_version
nestedProperties.description: S3 object version of the plugin archive

nestedProperties.name: content_type
nestedProperties.description: Content type of the plugin archive

properties.name: connector_configuration
properties.description: Kafka Connect connector configuration properties (key-value pairs, plugin-specific)

properties.name: kafka_connect_version
properties.description: Kafka Connect version (default 3.7.1, set by ERv2 module)

properties.name: capacity
properties.description: Connector capacity configuration (default provisioned 1 MCU 1 worker, set by ERv2 module)

properties.name: worker_configuration
properties.description: Optional multiline worker configuration properties

properties.name: log_delivery
properties.description: Log delivery configuration (optional)

properties.name: payerAccount
properties.description: AWS parent account

properties.name: ou
properties.description: AWS organizational unit. Use path to the OU, e.g. /Root/OrgUnit1/Foo/Bar

properties.name: tags
properties.description: Tags for this account

properties.name: mandatory
properties.description: is policy mandatory in the account

nestedProperties.name: serviceCode
nestedProperties.description: Specifies the service identifier. To find the service code value for an Amazon Web Services service, use the ListServices operation.

nestedProperties.name: quotaCode
nestedProperties.description: Specifies the quota identifier. To find the quota code for a specific quota, use the ListServiceQuotas operation, and look for the QuotaCode response in the output for the quota you want.

nestedProperties.name: value
nestedProperties.description: Specifies the new, increased value for the quota.

properties.name: auth
properties.description: Authentication configuration for the proxy. We currently only support auth via secret, hence the required fields.

nestedProperties.name: auth_scheme
nestedProperties.description: The type of authentication that the proxy uses for connections from the proxy to the underlying database

nestedProperties.name: client_password_auth_type
nestedProperties.description: The type of authentication the proxy uses for connections from clients

nestedProperties.name: description
nestedProperties.description: A user-specified description about the authentication used by a proxy to log in as a specific database user

nestedProperties.name: iam_auth
nestedProperties.description: Whether to require or allow AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) authentication for connections to the proxy

nestedProperties.name: secret_name
nestedProperties.description: Secret name that the proxy uses to authenticate to the RDS DB instance or Aurora DB cluster

nestedProperties.name: username
nestedProperties.description: The name of the database user to which the proxy connects

properties.name: connection_borrow_timeout
properties.description: The number of seconds for a proxy to wait for a connection to become available in the connection pool. Terraform attribute: connection_borrow_timeout

properties.name: db_instance_identifier
properties.description: DB instance identifier. The identifier of the RDS DB instance target

properties.name: debug_logging
properties.description: Whether the proxy includes detailed information about SQL statements in its logs. Default: false

properties.name: engine_family
properties.description: The kinds of databases that the proxy can connect to. This value determines which database network protocol the proxy recognizes when it interprets network traffic to and from the database

properties.name: iam_role_force_detach_policies
properties.description: Specifies to force detaching any policies the IAM role has before destroying it. Default: true

properties.name: iam_role_max_session_duration
properties.description: The maximum session duration (in seconds) for the IAM role. Default: 43200

properties.name: idle_client_timeout
properties.description: The number of seconds that a connection to the proxy can be inactive before the proxy disconnects it. Default: 1800

properties.name: init_query
properties.description: One or more SQL statements for the proxy to run when opening each new database connection

properties.name: log_group_retention_in_days
properties.description: Specifies the number of days you want to retain log events in the CloudWatch log group. Default: 30

properties.name: max_connections_percent
properties.description: The maximum size of the connection pool for each target in a target group. Default: 90

properties.name: max_idle_connections_percent
properties.description: Controls how actively the proxy closes idle database connections in the connection pool. Default: 50

properties.name: require_tls
properties.description: A Boolean parameter that specifies whether Transport Layer Security (TLS) encryption is required for connections to the proxy. Default: true

properties.name: session_pinning_filters
properties.description: Each item in the list represents a class of SQL operations that normally cause all later statements in a session using a proxy to be pinned to the same underlying database connection

properties.name: vpc_security_group_ids
properties.description: One or more VPC security group IDs to associate with the new proxy

properties.name: vpc_subnet_ids
properties.description: One or more VPC subnet IDs to associate with the new proxy

aws/sharing-option-1.yml.description: define sharing options between aws accounts

properties.name: provider
properties.description: type of sharing to implement

properties.name: regex
properties.description: regex expression to filter items by

properties.name: repository
properties.description: GitLab repository URL

properties.name: ref
properties.description: commit SHA, not a branch like main or master

properties.name: projectPath
properties.description: path in the repo where the terraform files are located

properties.name: delete
properties.description: set to true to delete the repo

properties.name: requireFips
properties.description: whether this repo should be validated to ensure it is using FIPS endpoints for AWS

properties.name: tfVersion
properties.description: Which version of terraform binary to use

properties.name: forceRerunTimestamp
properties.description: force a rerun of tf-repo using ISO8601 format which can be grabbed with https://www.utctime.net/

properties.name: variables
properties.description: Vault paths defining where Terraform inputs are read from and where Terraform outputs are written to

nestedProperties.name: switchover_timeout
nestedProperties.description:
The amount of time, in seconds, for the switchover to complete.
If the switchover takes longer than the specified duration,
then any changes are rolled back, and no changes are made to the environments.
The default timeout period is 300 seconds (5 minutes).
This is the max allowed downtime, use it to plan maintenance window.

nestedProperties.name: allocated_storage
nestedProperties.description:
The amount of storage in gibibytes (GiB) to allocate for the green DB instance.
You can choose to increase or decrease the allocated storage on the green DB instance.
Range from 20GiB to 16TiB (20-16384).

properties.name: grafana_datasource
properties.description: is rds instance used as a grafana datasource

properties.name: enable_http2
properties.description: indicates whether HTTP/2 is enabled

properties.name: ingress_cidr_blocks
properties.description: ingress cidr blocks to allow for an alb

properties.name: managed_by_erv2
properties.description: Manage the resource with erv2

properties.name: delete
properties.description: Flag to delete the resource

properties.name: max_session_duration
properties.description:
Maximum session duration (in seconds) that you want to set for the specified role.
If you do not specify a value for this setting, the default maximum of one hour is applied.
This setting can have a value from 3600 (1 hour) to 43200 (12 hours).

properties.name: secret_format
properties.description:
A jinja2 templated object to define the shape of the secret to be created in Secrets
Manager instead of copying the vault secret as is.

properties.name: msk_cluster
properties.description: Identifier of the MSK cluster resource in the same externalResources block

properties.name: service_execution_role
properties.description: Identifier of the aws-iam-role resource in the same externalResources block

aws/terraform-resource-lifecycle-1.yml.description: meta-arguments available for all resource blocks regardless of type

properties.name: create_before_destroy
properties.description: create replacement object before the prior object is destroyed

properties.name: prevent_destroy
properties.description: reject with an error any plan that would destroy the object

properties.name: ignore_changes
properties.description: attributes to ignore when planning updates to the object

properties.name: cidr_block
properties.description: Reference to a network reservation file

nestedProperties.name: private
nestedProperties.description: Private subnets CIDR blocks.

nestedProperties.name: public
nestedProperties.description: Public subnets CIDR blocks.

nestedProperties.name: availability_zones
nestedProperties.description: A list of availability zones names in the region.

nestedProperties.name: private_subnet_tags
nestedProperties.description: Private subnets tags.

nestedProperties.name: public_subnet_tags
nestedProperties.description: Public subnets tags.

properties.name: vpc_tags
properties.description: Additional tags for the VPC.

cloudflare/account-1.yml.description:
Schema for defining a Cloudflare account managed via app-interface.
This schema captures all metadata, configuration, credentials, and
ownership information required to manage Cloudflare accounts in a
declarative and automated way. It supports integration with Terraform,
secure credential management, and compliance requirements such as
two-factor enforcement and deletion approvals.

properties.name: name
properties.description:
The unique name of the Cloudflare account. Used to identify the
account across the system and in automation.

properties.name: description
properties.description:
A human-readable description of the Cloudflare account, its
intended use, or any relevant context.

properties.name: accountOwners
properties.description:
A list of individuals or teams responsible for this Cloudflare
account. Each entry must include a name and email address for
contact and accountability.

nestedProperties.name: name
nestedProperties.description: The name of the account owner.

nestedProperties.name: email
nestedProperties.description: The email address of the account owner.

properties.name: apiCredentials
properties.description:
Reference to the Vault secret containing the API credentials for
this Cloudflare account. Used by automation and integrations to
securely access the Cloudflare API.

properties.name: enforceTwofactor
properties.description:
If true, enforces two-factor authentication for all users in this
Cloudflare account to meet security and compliance requirements.

cloudflare/account-defaults-1.yml.description: Cloudflare Account default file, used as input for er-cloudflare-account module.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Account name

properties.name: enforce_twofactor
properties.description: Indicates whether membership in this account requires that Two-Factor Authentication is enabled

nestedProperties.name: email
nestedProperties.description: The contact email address of the user

nestedProperties.name: roles
nestedProperties.description: Array of roles associated with this member. Note the value is role name like `Administrator Read Only`

cloudflare/dns-record-1.yml.description:
Schema for defining a DNS record in a Cloudflare-managed zone.
This schema supports all standard DNS record types, including
A, AAAA, CNAME, TXT, NS, MX, CAA, DS, and DNSKEY. It enables
declarative management of DNS records, including advanced DNSSEC
and proxying options, for automation and compliance in
app-interface-managed Cloudflare zones.

properties.name: identifier
properties.description:
The unique identifier for this DNS record. Used to reference
and manage the record within app-interface and automation.

properties.name: name
properties.description:
The DNS name for this record (e.g., 'www.example.com').
This is the hostname or subdomain to which the record applies.

properties.name: type
properties.description:
The DNS record type. Supported types include A, AAAA, CAA,
CNAME, TXT, NS, MX, DS (for DNSSEC signing key), and DNSKEY
(for DNSSEC key records).

properties.name: ttl
properties.description:
The time-to-live (TTL) for this DNS record, in seconds.
Determines how long the record is cached by resolvers.

properties.name: content
properties.description:
The content for this DNS record. For most record types, this
is the target hostname, IP address, or record data.

properties.name: data
properties.description:
Additional data for advanced DNS record types, such as DS
and DNSKEY records for DNSSEC. Includes algorithm, key tag,
flags, protocol, public key, digest type, digest, tag, and value.

nestedProperties.name: algorithm
nestedProperties.description: The algorithm used for DNSSEC records (DS/DNSKEY).

nestedProperties.name: key_tag
nestedProperties.description: The key tag for DNSSEC records.

nestedProperties.name: flags
nestedProperties.description: The flags field for DNSKEY records.

nestedProperties.name: protocol
nestedProperties.description: The protocol field for DNSKEY records.

nestedProperties.name: public_key
nestedProperties.description: The public key for DNSKEY records.

nestedProperties.name: digest_type
nestedProperties.description: The digest type for DS records.

nestedProperties.name: digest
nestedProperties.description: The digest value for DS records.

nestedProperties.name: tag
nestedProperties.description: The tag for CAA records.

nestedProperties.name: value
nestedProperties.description: The value for CAA records.

properties.name: proxied
properties.description:
If true, enables Cloudflare proxying for this DNS record.
Only applicable to certain record types (e.g., A, AAAA, CNAME).

properties.name: priority
properties.description: The priority for MX records. Lower values indicate higher priority.

cloudflare/dns-zone-defaults-1.yml.description: Cloudflare DNS Zone default file, used as input for er-cloudflare-zone module.

properties.name: name
properties.description: The domain name.

properties.name: plan
properties.description: The ID of the rate plan.

properties.name: type
properties.description:
A full zone implies that DNS is hosted with Cloudflare.
A partial zone is typically a partner-hosted zone or a CNAME setup.

cloudflare/rule-1.yml.description:
A rule defines a filter and an action to perform on the incoming requests that match the filter.
Doc: https://developers.cloudflare.com/ruleset-engine/about/rules/
Schema based on https://registry.terraform.io/providers/cloudflare/cloudflare/latest/docs/resources/ruleset#nestedatt--rules

properties.name: action
properties.description: The action to perform when the rule matches.

properties.name: expression
properties.description: The expression defining which traffic will match the rule.

properties.name: description
properties.description: An informative description of the rule.

properties.name: enabled
properties.description: Whether the rule should be executed.

properties.name: ref
properties.description: The reference of the rule (the rule's ID by default).

properties.name: action_parameters
properties.description: The parameters configuring the rule's action.

cloudflare/ruleset-1.yml.description:
A ruleset is an ordered set of rules that you can apply to traffic on the Cloudflare global network.
Rulesets belong to a phase and can only execute in the same phase.
Doc: https://developers.cloudflare.com/ruleset-engine/about/rulesets/
Schema based on https://registry.terraform.io/providers/cloudflare/cloudflare/latest/docs/resources/ruleset

properties.name: identifier
properties.description: The unique identifier for this ruleset.

properties.name: name
properties.description: The human-readable name of the ruleset.

properties.name: kind
properties.description: The kind of the ruleset.

dependencies/acs-instance-1.yml.description:
Schema for referencing an Advanced Cluster Security (ACS) instance as a dependency.
This schema enables app-interface to reference ACS instances for integration and
configuration purposes. It does not provision or manage the lifecycle of the ACS
instance itself. Use this schema to declare connection details, credentials, and
authentication provider configuration for ACS integrations.

properties.name: name
properties.description:
The unique name of the ACS instance. Used to identify and reference
this instance in app-interface and related integrations.

properties.name: description
properties.description:
A human-readable description of the ACS instance, its environment,
or any relevant operational context.

properties.name: url
properties.description:
The URL of the ACS API endpoint. Used by integrations and automation
to connect to the ACS instance.

properties.name: credentials
properties.description:
Reference to the Vault secret containing the credentials required to
authenticate to this ACS instance.

properties.name: authProvider
properties.description:
Configuration for the authentication provider used by this ACS instance.
Supports OIDC and other provider types for integration and access control.

nestedProperties.name: name
nestedProperties.description: The name of the authentication provider configured for this ACS instance.

nestedProperties.name: id
nestedProperties.description: The unique identifier of the authentication provider.

nestedProperties.name: kind
nestedProperties.description: The kind of authentication provider. Currently supports 'oidc'.

dependencies/container-image-mirror-1.ym.description:
Schema for referencing a container image mirror as a dependency.
This schema allows app-interface to declare external container image
mirrors for use in automation and deployment workflows. It does not
provision or manage the lifecycle of the mirror itself. Use this schema
to specify the mirror URL, credentials, and tag filtering for integration.

properties.name: url
properties.description:
The URL of the container image mirror. This is the registry or
repository endpoint from which images can be pulled.

properties.name: pullCredentials
properties.description:
Reference to the Vault secret containing credentials required to
pull images from this mirror. Used for authentication to private
or restricted mirrors.

properties.name: tags
properties.description:
A list of image tags to include from this mirror. Only images
matching these tags will be considered for use.

properties.name: tagsExclude
properties.description:
A list of image tags to exclude from this mirror. Images matching
these tags will be ignored, even if included by the 'tags' field.

dependencies/dependency-1.yml.description:
Schema for referencing an external or internal dependency in app-interface.
This schema enables teams to declare dependencies for integration, monitoring,
and documentation purposes. It does not provision or manage the lifecycle of
the dependency itself. Use this schema to describe the dependency's name,
operational model, statefulness, SLA, monitoring, and failure impact.

properties.name: name
properties.description:
The unique name of the dependency. Used to identify and reference
this dependency in app-interface and related integrations.

properties.name: description
properties.description:
A human-readable description of the dependency, its purpose,
or any relevant operational context.

properties.name: statefulness
properties.description:
The statefulness classification of the dependency. 'Durable' indicates
persistent state, 'Cache' indicates ephemeral state, and 'Stateless'
indicates no state is maintained.

properties.name: opsModel
properties.description:
The operational model for the dependency. 'Hosted' means managed by a
third party, 'External' means outside the organization, and 'Internal'
means managed within the organization.

properties.name: statusPage
properties.description:
The URL of the status page for this dependency, if available. Used for
monitoring uptime and incident reporting.

properties.name: SLA
properties.description:
The service level agreement (SLA) for this dependency. Defines the
expected reliability, uptime, and support commitments.

properties.name: dependencyFailureImpact
properties.description:
The impact on your service if this dependency fails. Used for risk
assessment and incident response planning.

properties.name: monitoring
properties.description:
Monitoring configuration for this dependency. Supports specifying a
direct monitoring URL or referencing an OpenShift namespace and
resource for monitoring integration.

nestedProperties.name: url
nestedProperties.description: The direct URL to the monitoring dashboard or endpoint for this dependency.

nestedProperties.name: namespace
nestedProperties.description:
Reference to the OpenShift namespace where monitoring resources for
this dependency are located.

nestedProperties.name: provider
nestedProperties.description:
The provider type for monitoring. 'resource' for direct resource
monitoring, 'resource-template' for template-based monitoring.

nestedProperties.name: path
nestedProperties.description: The path to the monitoring resource or template.

properties.name: _target_namespace_zone
properties.description: a namespace and a route53 zone name provisioned within that namespace

properties.name: allowed_vault_secret_paths
properties.description: List of paths used to limit the secrets that can be accessed for creating DNS records, the primary use case being domain control validation (DCV)

properties.name: environmentID
properties.description: Dynatrace tenant ID.

properties.name: environmentUrl
properties.description: The URL to a specific Dynatrace entity called environment, which is also known as tenant.

properties.name: bootstrapToken
properties.description: Dynatrace API token dedicated for generating new API tokens for Hypershift.

properties.name: environment
properties.description: Which of integration, stage or production are the data coming from.

properties.description: https://gitlab.cee.redhat.com/service/app-interface/-/blame/9f303046645ebbb48e87ccf5d89778521a2d8345/data/services/osd-operators/cicd/saas/saas-dynatrace-operator.yaml#L356-433

properties.name: awsRegion
properties.description: A list of HCP AWS regions.

dependencies/endpoint-monitoring-provide.description:
Schema for referencing an endpoint monitoring provider as a dependency in app-interface.
This schema enables teams to declare monitoring providers for endpoints, supporting
integration with blackbox-exporter and signalfx. Use this schema to specify provider type,
check intervals, timeouts, metric labels, and provider-specific configuration for
endpoint monitoring automation and observability.

properties.name: name
properties.description:
The unique name of the endpoint monitoring provider. Used to identify and
reference this provider in app-interface and related integrations.

properties.name: description
properties.description:
A human-readable description of the endpoint monitoring provider, its
purpose, or any relevant operational context.

properties.name: provider
properties.description:
The provider defines which monitoring implementation will be used for endpoints.
Supported values are 'blackbox-exporter' and 'signalfx'.

properties.name: timeout
properties.description:
Timeout for the monitoring check (e.g., '10s', '5m'). Specifies how long
to wait for a check to complete before considering it failed.

properties.name: checkInterval
properties.description:
Interval between endpoint checks (e.g., '10s', '1m'). Determines how
frequently the monitoring provider will check the endpoint.

properties.name: metricLabels
properties.description:
Labels to be added to the resulting Prometheus metrics. Used for
categorization and filtering of metrics in observability systems.

properties.name: blackboxExporter
properties.description:
Configuration options specific to the blackbox-exporter provider.
Used when 'provider' is set to 'blackbox-exporter'.

nestedProperties.name: module
nestedProperties.description:
The blackbox exporter module to use for the endpoint check (e.g., 'http_2xx').
Modules define the type of probe and protocol to use.

nestedProperties.name: namespace
nestedProperties.description: Reference to the namespace where the Prometheus Probe CR will be created.

nestedProperties.name: exporterUrl
nestedProperties.description:
The blackbox-exporter URL to use. Must be a full scrape URL accessible
by the monitoring system.

properties.name: signalFx
properties.description:
Configuration options specific to the signalfx provider.
Used when 'provider' is set to 'signalfx'.

nestedProperties.name: namespace
nestedProperties.description: Reference to the namespace where the Prometheus Probe CR will be created.

nestedProperties.name: exporterUrl
nestedProperties.description:
The signalfx-prometheus-exporter URL to use. Must be a full scrape URL
accessible by the monitoring system.

nestedProperties.name: targetFilterLabel
nestedProperties.description: The signalfx label that holds the target value to be filtered on.

vault-config/audit-1.yml.description:
Schema for configuring a Vault audit device. Vault audit devices record
all requests and responses to Vault, providing a detailed audit trail for
security and compliance. This schema supports configuration of file, syslog,
and socket audit backends, allowing teams to specify device type, options,
and integration with Vault instances. Use this schema to ensure all Vault
operations are logged according to organizational and regulatory requirements.

properties.name: _path
properties.description:
The mount path for the audit device in Vault. Must end with a slash.
This determines where the audit device is enabled and how logs are
referenced in Vault.

properties.name: type
properties.description:
The type of audit device to enable. Supported types are 'file' for
writing logs to disk, 'syslog' for sending logs to the system logger,
and 'socket' for streaming logs to a network socket.

properties.name: instance
properties.description: Reference to the Vault instance where this audit device will be enabled.

properties.name: description
properties.description:
A human-readable description of the audit device configuration, its
purpose, or any relevant operational context.

properties.name: options
properties.description:
Configuration options specific to the audit device type. These options
control how and where audit logs are written, their format, and other
backend-specific settings. Refer to the Vault audit device documentation
for details on each option.

vault-config/auth-1.yml.description:
Schema for configuring a Vault authentication method. Vault auth methods
enable users, applications, and systems to authenticate and obtain tokens
for accessing secrets and APIs. This schema supports configuration of
multiple auth backends, including AppRole, GitHub, OIDC, and Kubernetes,
and allows specifying backend-specific settings, policy mappings, and
integration with Vault instances. Use this schema to ensure secure,
auditable, and flexible authentication to Vault in accordance with
organizational and regulatory requirements.

properties.name: _path
properties.description:
The mount path for the auth method in Vault. Must end with a slash.
Determines where the auth backend is enabled and how it is referenced.

properties.name: type
properties.description:
The type of Vault auth method to enable. Supported types include:
  - 'approle': Machine-to-machine authentication using AppRole IDs and secrets.
  - 'github': User authentication via GitHub organizations and teams.
  - 'oidc': User authentication via OpenID Connect providers.
  - 'kubernetes': Service account authentication from Kubernetes clusters.

properties.name: instance
properties.description: Reference to the Vault instance where this auth method will be enabled.

properties.name: description
properties.description:
A human-readable description of the auth method configuration, its
purpose, or any relevant operational context.

properties.name: settings
properties.description:
Backend-specific configuration for the auth method. Each auth type
supports different settings, such as OIDC discovery URLs, GitHub
organizations, or Kubernetes API endpoints. Refer to the Vault
documentation for details on each backend's configuration options.

properties.name: policy_mappings
properties.description:
A list of mappings from external identity groups (such as GitHub teams)
to Vault policies. Each mapping specifies the external group and the
Vault policies to assign upon successful authentication. This enables
fine-grained access control and policy assignment based on identity
provider attributes.

nestedProperties.name: github_team
nestedProperties.description: Reference to the GitHub team used for mapping to Vault policies.

nestedProperties.name: policies
nestedProperties.description:
A list of Vault policies to assign to users authenticated via this
external group.

vault-config/instance-1.yml.description:
Schema for defining a Vault instance managed by app-interface and vault-manager.
This schema enables configuration of multiple Vault clusters, supporting
environments with more than one Vault deployment. Each instance specifies
connection details, authentication, and operational metadata, allowing
vault-manager to manage audit devices, auth methods, policies, and secrets
for each Vault instance independently.

properties.name: name
properties.description:
The unique name of the Vault instance. Used to reference this instance
from other Vault configuration objects.

properties.name: description
properties.description:
A human-readable description of the Vault instance, its environment,
or any relevant operational context.

properties.name: address
properties.description:
The base URL or address of the Vault API endpoint for this instance.
Used by vault-manager and integrations to connect to the Vault cluster.

properties.name: auth
properties.description:
Reference to the authentication configuration for this Vault instance.
Specifies how vault-manager and integrations authenticate to this Vault
cluster, such as using a token or AppRole credentials.

properties.name: replication
properties.description:
A list of replication configurations for this Vault instance. Used to
define replication relationships (such as primary/secondary) between
Vault clusters for high availability and disaster recovery.

vault-config/instance-auth-1.yml.description:
Schema for defining authentication configuration for a Vault instance.
This schema supports multiple authentication providers, such as AppRole
and token-based authentication, and allows specifying the secret engine
version and required credentials. Use this schema to securely configure
how vault-manager and integrations authenticate to each Vault cluster.

properties.name: provider
properties.description:
The authentication provider to use for this Vault instance.
Supported values are 'approle' for AppRole-based authentication
and 'token' for token-based authentication.

properties.name: kubeRoleName
properties.description:
The name of the Kubernetes role to use for authentication, if
applicable. Used when integrating Vault with Kubernetes service
accounts.

properties.name: secretEngine
properties.description:
The version of the Vault KV secret engine to use for storing and
retrieving secrets. Must be either 'kv_v1' or 'kv_v2'.

properties.name: roleID
properties.description:
Reference to the Vault secret containing the AppRole RoleID.
Required when using the 'approle' provider.

properties.name: secretID
properties.description:
Reference to the Vault secret containing the AppRole SecretID.
Required when using the 'approle' provider.

properties.name: token
properties.description:
Reference to the Vault secret containing the authentication token.
Required when using the 'token' provider.

vault-config/policy-1.yml.description:
Schema for defining a Vault policy. Vault policies specify access control
rules for secrets, paths, and operations within a Vault instance. This
schema enables teams to manage policies declaratively, associating them
with specific Vault instances and providing fine-grained permissions for
users, applications, and integrations. Policies are written in HCL and
control what actions are allowed or denied within Vault.

properties.name: name
properties.description:
The unique name of the Vault policy. Used to identify and reference
this policy within the Vault instance and app-interface.

properties.name: instance
properties.description:
Reference to the Vault instance where this policy will be applied.
This enables management of policies across multiple Vault clusters.

properties.name: rules
properties.description:
The policy rules written in HCL (HashiCorp Configuration Language).
These rules define the allowed and denied actions for paths and
operations in Vault. Refer to the Vault documentation for policy
syntax and examples.

vault-config/replication-1.yml.description:
Schema for defining Vault replication configuration. Replication enables
secrets and data to be synchronized between multiple Vault instances,
supporting high availability, disaster recovery, and multi-region
deployments. This schema allows specifying the source and destination
authentication, the Vault instance, and the paths to replicate.

properties.name: vaultInstance
properties.description:
Reference to the Vault instance participating in replication.
This is the target instance where replicated data will be managed.

properties.name: destAuth
properties.description:
Authentication configuration for the destination Vault instance.
Specifies how to authenticate when writing replicated data.

properties.name: sourceAuth
properties.description:
Authentication configuration for the source Vault instance.
Specifies how to authenticate when reading data to replicate.

properties.name: paths
properties.description:
A list of paths to replicate between Vault instances. Each entry
specifies a path and its replication configuration.

vault-config/replication-paths-1.yml.description:
Schema for defining replication paths for Vault replication. Replication
paths specify which secrets or data should be synchronized between Vault
instances, and how access is managed for each path. This schema supports
integration with Jenkins instances and Vault policies, enabling fine-grained
control over which secrets are replicated and under what conditions.

properties.name: provider
properties.description:
The provider type for this replication path. Supported values are
'jenkins' for Jenkins integration and 'policy' for policy-based
replication.

properties.name: jenkinsInstance
properties.description:
Reference to the Jenkins instance associated with this replication
path. Required when the provider is 'jenkins'.

properties.name: policy
properties.description:
Reference to the Vault policy that governs access to this replication
path. Required for both 'jenkins' and 'policy' providers.

vault-config/role-1.yml.description:
Schema for defining a Vault role. Vault roles are used to configure
authentication and authorization for users, applications, or services
accessing secrets in Vault. This schema supports multiple auth backends
(AppRole, OIDC, Kubernetes), allows specifying policies, token settings,
and advanced options for fine-grained access control. Roles are associated
with a specific Vault instance and mount, and can be customized for
different use cases and security requirements.

properties.name: name
properties.description:
The unique name of the Vault role. Used to identify and reference
this role within the Vault instance and app-interface.

properties.name: mount
properties.description:
Reference to the Vault auth mount where this role is defined.
Specifies the authentication backend (e.g., approle, oidc, kubernetes).

properties.name: type
properties.description:
The type of Vault role. Indicates the authentication backend
(e.g., approle, oidc, kubernetes) this role is associated with.

properties.name: instance
properties.description:
Reference to the Vault instance where this role will be created.
Enables management of roles across multiple Vault clusters.

properties.name: output_path
properties.description:
The output path for generated credentials or tokens, if applicable.
Used for integrations that need to consume credentials from Vault.

properties.name: options
properties.description:
Configuration options specific to the role type and authentication
backend. Includes policy assignments, token settings, and advanced
options for AppRole, OIDC, and Kubernetes roles. Refer to the Vault
documentation for details on each backend's role configuration.

nestedProperties.name: policies
nestedProperties.description:
A list of Vault policies to attach to this role. Policies define
the permissions granted to tokens issued for this role.

nestedProperties.name: token_policies
nestedProperties.description: Additional policies to attach to tokens issued by this role.

vault-config/secret-engine-1.yml.description:
Schema for defining a Vault secret engine. Secret engines are components
in Vault that store, generate, or encrypt data, such as key-value (kv)
stores or TOTP generators. This schema allows configuring the mount path,
engine type, Vault instance, and backend-specific options. Use this schema
to declaratively manage secret engines across multiple Vault clusters.

properties.name: _path
properties.description:
The mount path for the secret engine in Vault. Must end with a slash.
Determines where the engine is enabled and how it is referenced.

properties.name: type
properties.description:
The type of secret engine to enable. Supported types are 'kv' for
key-value secrets and 'totp' for time-based one-time password generators.

properties.name: instance
properties.description:
Reference to the Vault instance where this secret engine will be enabled.
Enables management of secret engines across multiple Vault clusters.

properties.name: description
properties.description:
A human-readable description of the secret engine, its purpose, or
